comments.body:
📝 Walkthrough ## Walkthrough This PR adds a configurable demo mode that defaults to paper trading: env var documented, CLI parses it, app factory stores it on app.state, templates receive it via context helpers, UI shows a demo banner when enabled, and API broker construction is factored into a helper. ## Changes **Demo Mode Feature** | Layer / File(s) | Summary | |---|---| | **Configuration & CLI Parsing**
`\.env.example`, `src/agentic_portfolio/cli.py`, `tests/test_cli.py` | `DEMO_MODE` environment variable documented as optional with fail-safe default. `_parse_demo_mode()` returns `True` for unset/unrecognized values and `False` only for explicit false-y strings. `serve` command reads, parses, logs, and forwards the flag to `create_app()`. | | **App Factory & State Setup**
`src/agentic_portfolio/web/app.py` | `create_app()` signature extended with `demo_mode: bool = True`. Module-level logging added; app state stores `demo_mode` and startup log emits paper vs real execution message. | | **Template Context Preparation**
`src/agentic_portfolio/web/views.py` | New `_demo_context()` helper exposes `app.state.demo_mode` (defaulting to `True`) to templates. `_decision_detail_context()` and `_full_context()` are enriched with demo mode and a `"now"` UTC timestamp. | | **UI Styling & Banner Rendering**
`src/agentic_portfolio/web/static/style.css`, `src/agentic_portfolio/web/templates/base.html`, `tests/test_web_views.py` | CSS `.demo-banner` and related classes style an amber-on-black warning banner. Template conditionally renders "DEMO MODE" banner when `demo_mode=True`. Tests verify banner presence/absence based on flag state. | | **API Infrastructure for Broker Selection**
`src/agentic_portfolio/web/api.py` | New `_make_broker(request)` factory helper centralizes broker construction and documents future demo/live broker selection based on `app.state.demo_mode`. `_approve()` now delegates broker creation to this helper. | ## Estimated code review effort 🎯 3 (Moderate) | ⏱️ ~20 minutes ## Poem > 🐰 A banner now waves in the demo domain, > Paper trades whisper instead of pain. > From env to CLI, into app and view, > A gentle flag steers what we do. > Hop, test, and cheer — demo mode is true.
🚥 Pre-merge checks | ✅ 4 | ❌ 1 ### ❌ Failed checks (1 warning) | Check name | Status | Explanation | Resolution | | :----------------: | :--------- | :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | :--------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Docstring Coverage | ⚠️ Warning | Docstring coverage is 52.63% which is insufficient. The required threshold is 80.00%. | Write docstrings for the functions missing them to satisfy the coverage threshold. |
✅ Passed checks (4 passed) | Check name | Status | Explanation | | :------------------------: | :------- | :--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Title check | ✅ Passed | The title 'feat(security): add DEMO_MODE flag with fail-safe default' directly and clearly summarizes the main feature being added—a DEMO_MODE environment flag with fail-safe default behavior. | | Description check | ✅ Passed | The description is comprehensive and follows the template structure with all required sections completed: Summary, Type (Feature selected), Changes (detailed breakdown by category), Testing (test cases listed with pass status), and Checklist (all items checked). | | Linked Issues check | ✅ Passed | Check skipped because no linked issues were found for this pull request. | | Out of Scope Changes check | ✅ Passed | Check skipped because no linked issues were found for this pull request. |
✏️ Tip: You can configure your own custom pre-merge checks in the settings.
✨ Finishing Touches
📝 Generate docstrings - [ ] Create stacked PR - [ ] Commit on current branch
🧪 Generate unit tests (beta) - [ ] Create PR with unit tests - [ ] Commit unit tests in branch `feat/demo-mode`
--- Comment `@coderabbitai help` to get the list of available commands and usage tips.

reviews.body:
**Actionable comments posted: 3**

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

Inline comments:
In `@src/agentic_portfolio/cli.py`:
- Around line 375-413: The file fails the ruff formatter check; run the
repository formatter (e.g., ruff format or your project's pre-commit formatting
command) on src/agentic_portfolio/cli.py (or the whole repo) to fix style issues
around the demo_mode/logging block and function _parse_demo_mode, then re-run
ruff format --check; ensure no semantic changes to create_app invocation,
uvicorn.run, or the _parse_demo_mode implementation—only formatting changes.
- Around line 383-391: The startup warning printed by console.print when
demo_mode is False is misleading because broker construction is still
paper-only; update the message in the demo_mode conditional to reflect that
DEMO_MODE=false sets the runtime flag but the current broker implementation
remains paper trading (e.g., change the string in the console.print call for the
False branch to something like: "DEMO_MODE=false — live-trade flag set; however
the broker is currently configured for paper trading. Verify broker credentials
and risk controls before enabling real-money execution."). Ensure you only
change the printed text in the demo_mode False branch (the console.print call)
so it accurately warns about the current paper-only broker configuration while
prompting verification.

In `@src/agentic_portfolio/web/app.py`:
- Around line 71-76: The logger.info call formatting around the demo_mode
message is causing ruff format to fail; fix by reformatting the logger.info
invocation (the call that references logger.info and demo_mode) so the arguments
and the inline conditional string are properly wrapped and aligned per formatter
rules (e.g., place the format string and each argument on their own lines or
collapse to one line) and ensure trailing commas and parentheses are consistent
so ruff format/check passes.
```

</details>
